title: Autumn Quinoa Bowl
subtitle: with Brussels Slaw and Warm Coconut "Bacon"
description: Coconut "bacon" is one of our recent favorites. We use just a bit of liquid smoke, maple, and miso to amp up the crispy texture of the coconut and add a savory twist. You can reuse the tahini dressing recipe for future greens and grains — it's easy to make and delicious.

steps.title: COOK THE QUINOA
steps.text: Preheat oven to 300°F and line a baking sheet with parchment paper. In a small saucepan, combine the quinoa, 1¼ cups water, and a pinch of salt and place over high heat. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to a simmer, cover, and cook quinoa until water is absorbed and spirals start to burst, about 12 to 15 minutes.

steps.title: PREPARE THE SLAW
steps.text: Chop the walnuts. Place a small skillet over medium-high heat and add the walnuts. Cook until toasted, shaking the pan occasionally, about 3 to 5 minutes, and remove from heat. Thinly slice the Brussels sprouts, scallion, and apple and add to a large bowl along with the toasted walnuts and napa cabbage.

steps.title: MAKE THE COCONUT "BACON"
steps.text: Line a baking sheet with parchment paper. In a bowl, mix the miso paste, liquid smoke, a pinch of pepper, 1 tsp olive oil, and just 2 tsp maple syrup into a paste. Place the coconut chips on the baking sheet and toss them in the miso mixture, adding a little at a time until coated — you may not need all of the miso mixture. Bake until brown, stirring halfway to avoid sticking, about 5 to 8 minutes.

steps.title: MIX THE TAHINI DRESSING
steps.text: In a small bowl, combine the tahini, apple cider vinegar, and remaining 1 tsp maple syrup. Add a pinch of salt and 3 tbsp water. Whisk tahini dressing until smooth.

steps.title: TOSS THE SLAW
steps.text: Add the tahini dressing to the Brussels slaw. Season with salt and pepper and toss slaw well to combine.

steps.title: BUILD YOUR BOWLS
steps.text: Divide the cooked quinoa between large bowls. Top with Brussels slaw and warm coconut "bacon." Sprinkle with pomegranate seeds and dig in!

tips: *This item will be used in multiple steps.
TIP: If you have one, use a mandoline to slice your sprouts.
TIP: The coconut bacon will crisp up as it cools.
